MOTU UltraLite-mk3

Section-based notes for routing, CueMix, converters, monitoring, and hardware setup.

  • Overview
  • Hybrid FireWire/USB2
  • Mic/Guitar Inputs
  • Comprehensive I/O
  • Mixing and effects
  • CueMix FX software
  • Classic reverb
  • Modeled analog EQ
  • Vintage compression
  • Tuner & analysis tools
  • Clock and sync
  • MIDI
  • Expansion & drivers
  • Feature Summary

Modeled Analog EQ

Source: MOTU product page

The Modeled EQ is a 7-band parametric equalizer with filter curves modeled after British analog console EQ circuitry (drawing from the character of Neve and SSL-style shelving). Apply it to any input or output bus in CueMix FX with zero computer CPU usage.

Band Layout

  • High-Pass Filter: removes low-frequency content below the set frequency — essential for cutting rumble and proximity effect on the SM58.
  • Low Shelf: broad boost or cut below a set frequency — useful for managing bass weight.
  • 3 Parametric Bands: frequency-selectable, adjustable Q (bandwidth), boost or cut. The workhorse of corrective and creative EQ.
  • High Shelf: broad boost or cut above a set frequency — add "air" to a recording or tame harshness.
  • Low-Pass Filter: removes content above the set frequency — use for darkening a signal or removing high-frequency noise.

SM58 Vocal Dial-In Starting Point

  • Hi-pass filter at 80 Hz — removes proximity effect buildup and room rumble.
  • Parametric band at 300–500 Hz, −1 to −2 dB — cleans up the "boxy" midrange that dynamic mics can exhibit in close placement.
  • Parametric band at 3–5 kHz, +1 to +2 dB — enhances presence and vocal intelligibility.
  • High shelf at 10–12 kHz, +1 dB — adds subtle air and openness to the top end.

Hydrasynth Line Input Dial-In

  • Hi-pass at 30 Hz — sub protection; synthesizers can output sub-sonic content that wastes headroom.
  • Low shelf at 50–100 Hz, −1 dB — reduces low-end buildup if the mix already has bass coverage.
  • High shelf at 10 kHz, +1 dB — adds air to pad and lead sounds, improving definition in a busy mix.

Built for fast lookups, cleaner routing decisions, and practical studio recall.
